sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Simple lateral patellar dislocation may be reducible prehospital when the diagnosis is clear, the patient has no obvious fracture pattern, and reduction is clinically worthwhile. The aim is to relieve pain and restore extension without repeated forceful attempts.

sections.title: Reduction Technique
sections.content:
1. Confirm the patella appears laterally displaced and assess distal neurovascular status

2. Provide analgesia and support the patient in a relaxed supine position

3. Gently extend the knee while applying controlled medial pressure to guide the patella back into place

4. Stop if resistance is marked, pain is escalating, or the diagnosis becomes uncertain

5. After successful reduction, reassess neurovascular status and support the knee in extension or the most appropriate transport position

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Good analgesia and calm technique improve success more than force does
- Do not attempt repeated reductions when a fracture, locked knee, or complex injury is possible
- Document the diagnosis, analgesia used, number of attempts, and pre/post neurovascular findings
